content.description: Requirement checklist, timeline, root causes, solution plans in dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813/analysis.md; regression tests; changelog fragment; REQUIREMENTS.md update; run local CI checks; update PR description; gh pr ready.

input.new_string:
# Issue #812: nothing validated the workflow definitions themselves, so a
      # mistyped `needs.<job>` reference, an undefined output or a malformed
      # expression could only be discovered by pushing and watching it fail --
      # and expression errors that evaluate to an empty string fail *open*,
      # skipping the guarded step with a green check. actionlint type-checks
      # `needs`/`steps`/`env` contexts and runs shellcheck over every `run:`
      # block. Pinned by version; `.github/actionlint.yaml` declares the runner
      # labels newer than this release knows about.
      - name: Lint GitHub Actions workflows
        env:
          ACTIONLINT_VERSION: 1.7.7
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          curl -fsSL \
            "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/releases/download/v${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}/actionlint_${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
            | tar xz -C /tmp actionlint
          /tmp/actionlint -color

      - name: Check formatting
        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check

content.content:
for dmg in "release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg"; do
            echo "Mounting DMG: $dmg"
            mount_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
            installed_app="$RUNNER_TEMP/formal-ai Desktop.app"

            hdiutil attach "$dmg" -mountpoint "$mount_dir" -nobrowse -readonly
            mounted_app="$mount_dir/formal-ai Desktop.app"

            if [ ! -d "$mounted_app" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted DMG does not contain formal-ai Desktop.app."
              find "$mount_dir" -maxdepth 2 -print
              hdiutil detach "$mount_dir"
              exit 1
            fi

            rm -rf "$installed_app"
            ditto "$mounted_app" "$installed_app"
            hdiutil detach "$mount_dir"

            app_info="$installed_app/Contents/Info.plist"
            executable="$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :CFBundleExecutable' "$app_info")"
            bundle_id="$(/usr/libexec/PlistBuddy -c 'Print :CFBundleIdentifier' "$app_info")"

            if [ "$bundle_id" != "ai.formal.desktop" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Unexpected macOS app bundle identifier: $bundle_id"
              exit 1
            fi

            if [ ! -x "$installed_app/Contents/MacOS/$executable" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted app executable is missing or not executable."
              find "$installed_app/Contents" -maxdepth 3 -print
              exit 1
            fi

            if [ ! -f "$installed_app/Contents/_CodeSignature/CodeResources" ]; then
              echo "::error file=$dmg::Mounted app is missing its signed CodeResources envelope."
              find "$installed_app/Contents/_CodeSignature" -maxdepth 1 -print 2>/dev/null || true
              exit 1
            fi

            codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=2 "$installed_app"

            if [ "$MACOS_BUILD_MODE" = "signed" ]; then
              spctl --assess --type execute --verbose=4 "$installed_app"
              xcrun stapler validate "$installed_app"

content.content:
id: package
        working-directory: vscode
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # `vscode:prepublish` (prepare-resources.mjs) syncs the extension
          # version from Cargo.toml and copies src/web + data/seed + desktop/lib
          # into the package, so the .vsix is fully self-contained and its
          # filename (formal-ai-vscode-<version>.vsix) matches the release
          # version the /download and /vscode pages resolve.
          npm run package
          vsix="$(ls formal-ai-vscode-*.vsix 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"
          [ -n "$vsix" ] || { echo "::error::vsce package produced no .vsix" >&2; exit 1; }
          echo "vsix=$vsix" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Packaged: $vsix"

=== release 513
        run: |
          node --test $(ls desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs | grep -v '/web-tools.test.mjs$')

  # === TEST ===
  # Test runs independently of the changelog check and gates on the change
  # detector so it never burns runner time on non-code changes (issue #442).
  #
  # Previously this job ran whenever `changelog` was skipped. But `changelog`
  # is skipped precisely when there are NO code changes (docs-only / .gitkeep /
  # changelog-fragment-only commits), so `needs.changelog.result == 'skipped'`
  # turned "no code changed" into "run the full test suite" — the exact
  # opposite of what we want. Gate on the detect-changes outputs instead, the
  # same way `lint` and `coverage` already do.
=== release 1384
        run: |
          bun add -g @link-assistant/agent
          echo "$HOME/.bun/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH

      - name: Install external agent CLIs
        # OpenCode provides the second issue-715 learning harness. Issue #781
        # additionally proves the same research loop through Claude Code's
        # Anthropic transport and Codex's Responses transport, so all clients
        # are mandatory and their E2E scripts fail rather than skip if missing.

content.subject: Fix self-hosting metric denominator (non-authored artifacts)
content.description: 94.58% of measured changed lines in the v0.301.0 window are committed .log/.jsonl/.diff dumps under dev/log. Exclude captured artifacts and lockfiles from both numerator and denominator in scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs; add metric_version epoch so rows from different definitions are never compared.

content.subject: Move ratchet enforcement to where it is actionable
content.description: record_release aborts Auto Release on a ratchet fall — a non-actionable gate on immutable history that permanently blocks releases (3rd outage of this class). Add RatchetPolicy Enforce/Report; report+record at release time, enforce in the PR evidence-check job.

content.description: Build macos-x64/arm64 fail: codesign refuses the bundled Google Chrome for Testing Framework ("unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework"). Awaiting subagent root-cause report.

      - name: Run desktop packaging regression tests
        run: node --test desktop/scripts/adhoc-sign-mac.test.cjs desktop/scripts/prepare-resources.test.mjs

      # Issue #672 (F2): the desktop library tests were written but never run by
      # any job, so `desktop/lib/data-migration.cjs` — the code that moves a
      # user's whole profile between app names — had no gate at all. They are
      # plain node:test files over in-memory fakes, so they need no Electron and
      # no desktop dependency tree. `web-tools.test.mjs` is the one exception:
      # it imports @link-assistant/web-search through desktop/lib/web-tools.cjs,
      # which is only present after `npm ci` in desktop/, so it stays out of
      # this dependency-free job rather than being silently skipped inside it.
      - name: Run desktop library tests
        run: |
          node --test $(ls desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs | grep -v '/web-tools.test.mjs$')

input.old_string:
- name: Run desktop library tests
        run: |
          node --test $(ls desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs | grep -v '/web-tools.test.mjs$')
input.new_string:
- name: Run desktop library tests
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Issue #812: this was `node --test $(ls ... | grep -v ...)`. If the
          # glob ever stopped matching, the substitution collapsed to nothing and
          # `node --test` fell back to its own directory discovery -- a green
          # step that ran none of these tests. Build the list explicitly and
          # refuse to pass on an empty one.
          shopt -s nullglob
          tests=()
          for test_file in desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs; do
            case "$test_file" in
              */web-tools.test.mjs) continue ;;
            esac
            tests+=("$test_file")
          done
          if [ ${#tests[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::No desktop library tests matched desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          printf 'Running %s test file(s)\n' "${#tests[@]}"
          node --test "${tests[@]}"

/tmp/actionlint -color

      - name: Check formatting
input.new_string:
/tmp/actionlint -color

      # actionlint only reaches shell embedded in `run:` blocks. The standalone
      # scripts those blocks invoke -- install.sh, check-secrets.sh,
      # simulate-fresh-merge.sh and the rest -- had no linter at all, which is
      # where unquoted expansions and `cd` without a guard live (issue #812).
      # `experiments/` is deliberately excluded: those are throwaway
      # reproduction scripts, not shipped code.
      - name: Lint shell scripts
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mapfile -t scripts < <(
            git ls-files '*.sh' \
              | grep -v -e '^experiments/' -e '^dev/log/' -e '^docs/case-studies/'
          )
          if [ ${#scripts[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::No shell scripts matched; the lint selector is broken" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          printf 'Linting %s shell script(s)\n' "${#scripts[@]}"
          shellcheck --severity=warning "${scripts[@]}"

      - name: Check formatting

content.content:
20260720_120000_issue_810_release_evidence_deadlock.md
20260720_130000_issue_804_macos_sign_debug.md
20260720_140000_issue_673_workspace_ast_census.md
20260720_140000_issue_808_ci_cd_fixes.md
README.md
---
bump: patch
---

### Fixed
- Replaced the contradictory `always() && !cancelled()` job guards in the CI/CD pipeline with `!cancelled()`, so cancelling a run actually stops the dependent jobs (issue #808).
- Excluded the bundled Chrome for Testing runtime from macOS code signing via `electron-builder`'s `mac.signIgnore`, so ad-hoc desktop builds no longer fail with "unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework" (issue #808).
- Routed the macOS ad-hoc signing diagnostics to stderr and added an unconditional hook-entry line, so the sign trace actually reaches CI logs; the per-file trace stays default-off behind `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG` (issue #808).

- Set `CSC_FOR_PULL_REQUEST` for ad-hoc macOS packaging, so `electron-builder` no longer skips code signing on pull-request runs and the packaged app keeps its `CodeResources` envelope (issue #808).

### Added
- Desktop and VS Code extension packaging now run on pull requests that touch `desktop/`, `vscode/` or the release workflow, exercising the full six-target build matrix, macOS signing and the artifact smoke tests in dry-run mode with every publishing step skipped (issue #808).
- Pull-request job building the Docker image and running `scripts/verify-docker-runtime.sh` inside it, so a broken `Dockerfile` can no longer surface only after the crate has been published (issue #808).
- Pull-request job validating `Formal-AI-Session` / `Formal-AI-Evidence` commit trailers with the same measurement the release uses, so a malformed trailer is rejected before it can fail Auto Release on `main` (issue #808).
- Pull-request fresh-merge simulation (`scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, adopted from the pipeline templates), so a pull request cannot pass against a stale merge preview and then break `main` (issue #808).
- Secrets scan on pull requests (`scripts/check-secrets.sh`, secretlint recommended preset) covering the files a change touches (issue #808).
- Resilient Buildx setup composite action retrying the BuildKit image pull with a registry-mirror fallback, replacing the raw `docker/setup-buildx-action` uses (issue #808).

input.content:
---
bump: patch
---

### Fixed
- Stopped the self-hosting ratchet from deadlocking every release: the metric no longer counts captured CI transcripts (`*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.diff`, `*.patch`, `*.stderr`, `*.stdout`) or dependency lockfiles, which made up 94.20% of the measured range and turned "commit your evidence" into a guaranteed regression, and enforcement moved from `record_release` on `main` — where every contributing commit is already immutable — to a differential pull-request gate where the commits can still be amended (issue #812).
- `cargo clippy` now runs with `-D warnings`. Every lint in `[lints.clippy]` is set to `warn`, so the job printed findings and still exited 0 (issue #812).
- `auto-release` and `manual-release` now gate on `Secrets Scan` and both E2E suites, not just `[lint, test, build]`; a red secrets scan on `main` could previously publish the crate, the Docker image and the GitHub Release anyway (issue #812).
- The desktop `finalize` job runs under `!cancelled()` instead of `always()`, so a cancelled run can no longer clobber a complete `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial one via `gh release upload --clobber` (issue #812).
- The desktop packaging and VS Code jobs now simulate the fresh merge on pull requests, the same way `lint` and `test` already did, so packaging is validated against the merge result rather than a stale merge preview (issue #812).
- `scripts/check-file-size.rs` excluded `.github/workflows/**` by accident: its `.git` exclusion was matched as a substring. Directory exclusions are now matched per path component, GitHub Actions workflows are measured (warn at 1500 lines, fail at 2000), and quoted third-party workflows under `docs/case-studies/**` stay exempt (issue #812).
- `node --test $(ls … | grep -v …)` in the desktop library test step fell back to Node's own test discovery if the glob ever stopped matching — a green step running none of the intended tests. The file list is now built explicitly and an empty list fails (issue #812).
- Pinned `secretlint` to an explicit version in `scripts/check-secrets.sh`; `npx --yes -p secretlint` resolved to whatever `latest` was when the job ran (issue #812).
- Quoted `$BASE_REF` at every expansion in `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, which upstream leaves bare on two of three lines (issue #812).

### Added
- `actionlint` (with `shellcheck`) now lints every workflow definition, and `shellcheck` lints the shipped `*.sh` scripts. Nothing validated either before, so a mistyped `needs.<job>` reference or a malformed expression — which fails *open*, silently skipping the guarded step under a green check — could only be found by pushing (issue #812).
- `METRIC_VERSION` on self-hosting ledger rows, so a change to how the share is measured starts a new comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history; rows from different epochs are never compared (issue #812).

### Fixed
- Stopped the self-hosting ratchet from deadlocking every release: the metric no longer counts captured CI transcripts (`*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.diff`, `*.patch`, `*.stderr`, `*.stdout`) or dependency lockfiles, which made up 94.20% of the measured range and turned "commit your evidence" into a guaranteed regression, and enforcement moved from `record_release` on `main` — where every contributing commit is already immutable — to a differential pull-request gate where the commits can still be amended (issue #812).
- `cargo clippy` now runs with `-D warnings`. Every lint in `[lints.clippy]` is set to `warn`, so the job printed findings and still exited 0 (issue #812).
- `auto-release` and `manual-release` now gate on `Secrets Scan` and both E2E suites, not just `[lint, test, build]`; a red secrets scan on `main` could previously publish the crate, the Docker image and the GitHub Release anyway (issue #812).
- The desktop `finalize` job runs under `!cancelled()` instead of `always()`, so a cancelled run can no longer clobber a complete `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial one via `gh release upload --clobber` (issue #812).
- The desktop packaging and VS Code jobs now simulate the fresh merge on pull requests, the same way `lint` and `test` already did, so packaging is validated against the merge result rather than a stale merge preview (issue #812).
- `scripts/check-file-size.rs` excluded `.github/workflows/**` by accident: its `.git` exclusion was matched as a substring. Directory exclusions are now matched per path component, GitHub Actions workflows are measured (warn at 1500 lines, fail at 2000), and quoted third-party workflows under `docs/case-studies/**` stay exempt (issue #812).
- `node --test $(ls … | grep -v …)` in the desktop library test step fell back to Node's own test discovery if the glob ever stopped matching — a green step running none of the intended tests. The file list is now built explicitly and an empty list fails (issue #812).
- Pinned `secretlint` to an explicit version in `scripts/check-secrets.sh`; `npx --yes -p secretlint` resolved to whatever `latest` was when the job ran (issue #812).
- Quoted `$BASE_REF` at every expansion in `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, which upstream leaves bare on two of three lines (issue #812).

### Added
- `actionlint` (with `shellcheck`) now lints every workflow definition, and `shellcheck` lints the shipped `*.sh` scripts. Nothing validated either before, so a mistyped `needs.<job>` reference or a malformed expression — which fails *open*, silently skipping the guarded step under a green check — could only be found by pushing (issue #812).
- `METRIC_VERSION` on self-hosting ledger rows, so a change to how the share is measured starts a new comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history; rows from different epochs are never compared (issue #812).

file.content:
Git-history metric, a Links Notation release ledger, and a trailing-window
ratchet that starts honestly at 0%.

| ID | Requirement | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R473 | Define auditable Formal AI authorship from committed session evidence rather than author names or an unverified claim. | Implemented by paired `Formal-AI-Session` and `Formal-AI-Evidence` commit trailers. `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs` verifies the evidence exists in the attributed commit and contains the exact session id plus a `formal-ai` marker. |
| R474 | Measure release work deterministically as additions plus deletions across non-merge commits, excluding binary files. | Implemented with Git `rev-list` and `show --numstat --no-renames`; arithmetic uses integer basis points with deterministic half-up rounding. |
| R475 | Provide `rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since <tag>` and cover it with a fixture Git repository and ledger. | Implemented by the standalone script and `recorded_formal_ai_evidence_drives_the_release_metric_and_ratchet`, whose two-commit fixture proves an exact 3/4 or 75.00% result. |
| R476 | Record a self-hosting row for every release as committed Links Notation data. | `scripts/version-and-commit.rs` records the new tag's row in `data/meta/self-hosting-ledger.lino` before the release commit and stages it with the version artifacts. The initial `v0.296.0` row truthfully records 0%. |
| R477 | Include the recorded share in every GitHub release body. | Both automatic and manual paths in `.github/workflows/release.yml` pass the ledger to `scripts/create-github-release.rs`, which appends the exact tagged row under `## Self-hosting`. |
| R478 | Enforce a monotonic non-decreasing trailing-window ratchet while accepting an honest 0% starting point. | `record_release` computes a changed-line-weighted three-release share, rejects a decrease, and treats an empty history as 0%; the fixture test covers both first-row acceptance and regression rejection. |
| R479 | Document attribution, preserve issue/PR/research and Formal AI replay evidence, and pin pipeline integration with a specification test. | Implemented by `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `docs/case-studies/issue-657/`, and `release_pipeline_and_ledger_remain_pinned_to_the_metric`. |

content.subject: Fix self-hosting metric denominator (non-authored artifacts)
content.description: 94.58% of measured changed lines in the v0.301.0 window are committed .log/.jsonl/.diff dumps under dev/log. Exclude captured artifacts and lockfiles from both numerator and denominator in scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs; add metric_version epoch so rows from different definitions are never compared.

content.subject: Move ratchet enforcement to where it is actionable
content.description: record_release aborts Auto Release on a ratchet fall — a non-actionable gate on immutable history that permanently blocks releases (3rd outage of this class). Add RatchetPolicy Enforce/Report; report+record at release time, enforce in the PR evidence-check job.

| R478 | Enforce a monotonic non-decreasing trailing-window ratchet while accepting an honest 0% starting point. | `record_release` computes a changed-line-weighted three-release share, rejects a decrease, and treats an empty history as 0%; the fixture test covers both first-row acceptance and regression rejection. |

input.new_string:
| R478 | Enforce a monotonic non-decreasing trailing-window ratchet while accepting an honest 0% starting point. | `record_release` computes a changed-line-weighted three-release share and treats an empty history as 0%. Issue #812 moved *where* the decrease is rejected: at release time the ratchet is `RatchetPolicy::Report` (record the row, emit a `::warning`), because by then every contributing commit is immutable history on `main` and the only way to move the measured range forward — cutting a tag — is what a hard failure blocks. Enforcement is `RatchetPolicy::Enforce` at the pull-request gate (`--check-ratchet`), where commits can still be amended, and is differential: it compares the share projected *with* the branch against the one projected for the base alone, so a regression already on `main` cannot fail unrelated pull requests. Covered by `a_falling_ratchet_reports_at_release_time_instead_of_blocking_it` and `the_pull_request_gate_only_judges_the_branchs_own_delta`. |
| R479 | Document attribution, preserve issue/PR/research and Formal AI replay evidence, and pin pipeline integration with a specification test. | Implemented by `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `docs/case-studies/issue-657/`, and `release_pipeline_and_ledger_remain_pinned_to_the_metric`. |
| R480 | A change to how the share is measured must start a new comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history. | Implemented by `METRIC_VERSION` in `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs`: every ledger row carries the version that produced it, `rows` filters to the current epoch, and `parse_release_row` treats a row without the field as epoch 1. Rows from different epochs are never averaged or compared. The ledger header documents what each epoch measures. Covered by `rows_from_an_older_measurement_epoch_are_never_compared`. |

toolUseResult.newString:
| R478 | Enforce a monotonic non-decreasing trailing-window ratchet while accepting an honest 0% starting point. | `record_release` computes a changed-line-weighted three-release share and treats an empty history as 0%. Issue #812 moved *where* the decrease is rejected: at release time the ratchet is `RatchetPolicy::Report` (record the row, emit a `::warning`), because by then every contributing commit is immutable history on `main` and the only way to move the measured range forward — cutting a tag — is what a hard failure blocks. Enforcement is `RatchetPolicy::Enforce` at the pull-request gate (`--check-ratchet`), where commits can still be amended, and is differential: it compares the share projected *with* the branch against the one projected for the base alone, so a regression already on `main` cannot fail unrelated pull requests. Covered by `a_falling_ratchet_reports_at_release_time_instead_of_blocking_it` and `the_pull_request_gate_only_judges_the_branchs_own_delta`. |
| R479 | Document attribution, preserve issue/PR/research and Formal AI replay evidence, and pin pipeline integration with a specification test. | Implemented by `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `docs/case-studies/issue-657/`, and `release_pipeline_and_ledger_remain_pinned_to_the_metric`. |
| R480 | A change to how the share is measured must start a new comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history. | Implemented by `METRIC_VERSION` in `scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs`: every ledger row carries the version that produced it, `rows` filters to the current epoch, and `parse_release_row` treats a row without the field as epoch 1. Rows from different epochs are never averaged or compared. The ledger header documents what each epoch measures. Covered by `rows_from_an_older_measurement_epoch_are_never_compared`. |

file.content:
resolve:
    name: Resolve target release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: read
    # For workflow_run, continue after ANY completed main-branch pipeline except
    # cancelled/skipped runs (issue #479). We intentionally do NOT gate on
    # conclusion == 'success': the auto-release publishes the GitHub release in an
    # EARLY pipeline job, so a LATER job failing (e.g. the E2E Pages probe timing
    # out) still leaves a real, asset-less release that desktop assets must heal.
    # Gating on full-pipeline green meant one unrelated late failure suppressed
    # every desktop build -> "Not available in latest release". The actual
    # decision to build is delegated to scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh, whose
    # idempotency guard skips when the resolved release already carries desktop
    # assets, so running on a non-success conclusion is safe and self-healing.
    # Pull requests resolve nothing: there is no release to heal and no tag to
    # check out. The job is skipped and `build` admits the skipped dependency
    # explicitly, so the packaging matrix still runs against the PR head.
    if: >-
      github.event_name != 'pull_request' &&
      (github.event_name != 'workflow_run' ||
      (github.event.workflow_run.head_branch == 'main' &&
       github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'cancelled' &&
       github.event.workflow_run.conclusion != 'skipped'))
    outputs:
      tag: ${{ steps.pick.outputs.tag }}
      should_build: ${{ steps.pick.outputs.should_build }}
    steps:
      # Check out the repo so the resolve logic lives in a unit-tested script
      # (scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh) instead of being inlined here. The
      # script only needs to *exist*; it queries the GitHub API for everything
      # else, so checking out the default ref is sufficient.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

      - name: Resolve tag and whether desktop assets are needed
        id: pick
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
          EVENT: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_TAG: ${{ github.event.inputs.tag }}
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
          REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
          WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.workflow_run.head_sha }}
        # Issue #479: the automated release tags a CHILD "chore: release vX.Y.Z"
        # commit whose first parent is the completed CI head SHA, so matching the
        # tag to the head SHA directly always failed and desktop assets were
        # never built. desktop-release-resolve.sh resolves the latest release
        # instead (with a defensive exact-SHA tier and an idempotency guard) and
        # emits verbose ::group:: diagnostics for future debugging.
        run: bash scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh

  build:
    name: Build ${{ matrix.label }}
    needs: resolve

content.subject: Collect CI evidence into dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813
content.description: Download both failing run logs + job metadata, break down failures. DONE for ci-logs; still need issue/PR metadata JSON and the analysis writeup.

content.subject: Fix self-hosting metric denominator (non-authored artifacts)
content.description: 94.58% of measured changed lines in the v0.301.0 window are committed .log/.jsonl/.diff dumps under dev/log. Exclude captured artifacts and lockfiles from both numerator and denominator in scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs; add metric_version epoch so rows from different definitions are never compared.

content.subject: Move ratchet enforcement to where it is actionable
content.description: record_release aborts Auto Release on a ratchet fall — a non-actionable gate on immutable history that permanently blocks releases (3rd outage of this class). Add RatchetPolicy Enforce/Report; report+record at release time, enforce in the PR evidence-check job.

content.subject: Fix macOS desktop codesign failure
content.description: Build macos-x64/arm64 fail: codesign refuses the bundled Google Chrome for Testing Framework ("unsealed contents present in the root directory of an embedded framework"). Awaiting subagent root-cause report.

content.subject: Apply CI/CD template sweep findings
content.description: Requirement 7: compare against link-foundation rust/js/python pipeline templates and hive-mind CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md; apply fixes here and draft upstream reports. Awaiting subagent report.

content.subject: Write analysis, tests, changelog, and finalize PR 813
content.description: Requirement checklist, timeline, root causes, solution plans in dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813/analysis.md; regression tests; changelog fragment; REQUIREMENTS.md update; run local CI checks; update PR description; gh pr ready.

input.new_string:
emit_outputs() {
  log "result: tag='${tag}' should_build='${should_build}' resolution='${resolution}'"
  # Issue #812: a healing build checks out the *tag*, so it packages that tag's
  # code -- not `main`. Run 29752745259 failed macOS signing on v0.300.0, which
  # predates the four packaging fixes already merged, and the red run read as
  # "desktop packaging is broken" when it meant "the last tag is old". Say so in
  # the run summary rather than leaving it to be re-diagnosed each time.
  if [ "$should_build" = "true" ] && [ -n "$tag" ]; then
    head_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/HEAD" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    tag_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/${tag}" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [ -n "$head_sha" ] && [ -n "$tag_sha" ] && [ "$head_sha" != "$tag_sha" ]; then
      echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. This run builds that tag's code; fixes merged after it are not included, so a failure here does not necessarily mean current code is broken."
    fi
  fi
  if [ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]; then

      echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. This run builds that tag's code; fixes merged after it are not included, so a failure here does not necessarily mean current code is broken."
    fi
  fi
  if [ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]; then

lines: +  # Issue #812: a healing build checks out the *tag*, so it packages that tag's
+  # code -- not `main`. Run 29752745259 failed macOS signing on v0.300.0, which
+  # predates the four packaging fixes already merged, and the red run read as
+  # "desktop packaging is broken" when it meant "the last tag is old". Say so in
+  # the run summary rather than leaving it to be re-diagnosed each time.

+      echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. This run builds that tag's code; fixes merged after it are not included, so a failure here does not necessarily mean current code is broken."

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. **Primary Request and Intent:**

   One genuine user message directed solving GitHub issue https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/812 ("Check for all false positives, false negatives, warnings and errors in CI/CD and fix them all") in `/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784567326843`, on branch `issue-812-7f9674a82402`, updating existing PR https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/813.

   Explicit user requirements (verbatim intent):
   - "Download all logs and collect data related about the issue to this repository, and compile that data into the ./dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813 folder."
   - "Use the collected evidence to do a deep analysis (search online for additional facts and data), reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events, list each and every requirement from the issue, find the root cause of each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement. Also check online for known existing components/libraries that solve a similar problem or can help."
   - "If there is not enough data to find the actual root cause, add debug output and a verbose mode (if not already present) so the root cause can be found on the next iteration. Keep the default state switched off."
   - "If the issue is related to another repository/project, report issues on GitHub for that project when possible. Each report must contain reproducible examples, workarounds, and suggestions for fixing the issue in code."
   - "Double-check that the requirements are fully applied to the entire codebase: if an issue exists in multiple places, apply it in all of them."

   Environment/guideline constraints: push ONLY to `issue-812-7f9674a82402`; PR 813 already exists (update via `gh pr edit`, don't create); run local CI checks before pushing; commit atomic steps; `gh pr ready 813` when finished; save large logs to files; read files >1500 lines in chunks; keep debug/test scripts in `./examples` and/or `./experiments`.

   Issue-body requirements: compare the full file tree against `link-foundation/{rust,js,python}-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`; report the same defect upstream in the templates where present; follow `https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/blob/main/docs/CI-CD-BEST-PRACTICES.md`; "plan and execute everything in this single pull request … until it is each and every requirement fully addressed".

2. **Key Technical Concepts:**
   - GitHub Actions: `needs:`/`if:`/`timeout-minutes`, `!cancelled()` vs `always()`, `workflow_run` healing builds, `::warning`/`::notice`/`::error` annotations, `needs.<job>.result` values (success/failure/cancelled/skipped)
   - GitHub expression truthiness: numeric `0` is falsy, so `${{ cond && 0 || 1 }}` is wrong; must use quoted `'0'`/`'1'`
   - `rust-script` standalone scripts in `scripts/*.rs`, included into tests via `#[path = "../../../scripts/x.rs"] mod`
   - actionlint 1.7.7 (type-checks `needs`/`steps`/`env` contexts, embeds shellcheck over `run:` blocks); `.github/actionlint.yaml` `self-hosted-runner.labels`
   - shellcheck 0.10.0: SC2066 (quoted single-word for-loop), SC2012 (`ls` parsing), SC2046/SC2010 (`$(ls | grep)`), SC2086 (unquoted expansion), SC2164, SC2034, SC2064
   - Self-hosting metric: `Formal-AI-Session`/`Formal-AI-Evidence` trailers, basis-point arithmetic, trailing-window weighted ratchet, `RatchetPolicy::{Enforce, Report}`, `METRIC_VERSION` measurement epochs, differential (branch-vs-base) gating
   - `clippy::pedantic`/`nursery` with `[lints.clippy] all/pedantic/nursery = "warn"` — requires `-D warnings` at the CLI to actually gate
   - electron-builder / `@electron/osx-sign` ad-hoc codesigning; `validateOptsIgnore` array bug in v1.3.3
   - POSIX `sh` vs bash: `pipefail` is not POSIX (breaks `curl | sh` under dash)

3. **Files and Code Sections:**

   - **`scripts/check-file-size.rs`** — COMPLETED this session. The `.git` substring exclusion made every workflow invisible to the size gate.
     ```rust
     /// Issue #812: `EXCLUDE_PATTERNS` are directory *names*, so they are matched per
     /// path component. A substring test hid every workflow from the gate --
     /// `.github/workflows/release.yml` contains `.git` -- and would likewise skip
     /// any path with `target` or `node_modules` inside a longer segment.
     fn should_exclude(path: &Path) -> bool {
         let path_str = normalized_path(path);
         path_str
             .split('/')
             .any(|component| EXCLUDE_PATTERNS.contains(&component))
             || EXCLUDE_PATH_FRAGMENTS
                 .iter()
                 .any(|fragment| path_str.contains(fragment))
     }
     ```
     ```rust
     /// Only workflows this repository owns are measured. `docs/case-studies/**`
     /// holds verbatim copies of other projects' pipelines as evidence; reformatting
     /// them to fit our ceiling would destroy the thing they document.
     fn is_workflow_yaml_path(path: &Path) -> bool {
         let path_str = normalized_path(path);
         let has_yaml_extension = path
             .extension()
             .and_then(|extension| extension.to_str())
             .is_some_and(|extension| {
                 extension.eq_ignore_ascii_case("yml") || extension.eq_ignore_ascii_case("yaml")
             });
         has_yaml_extension && (path_str.starts_with(".github/") || path_str.contains("/.github/"))
     }

     fn file_limit(path: &Path) -> Option<&'static FileLimit> {
         if is_worker_js_path(path) { return Some(&WORKER_JS_LIMIT); }
         if is_workflow_yaml_path(path) { return Some(&WORKFLOW_YAML_LIMIT); }
         let ext = path.extension().and_then(|ext| ext.to_str())?;
         FILE_LIMITS.iter().find(|limit| limit.extension == ext)
     }
     ```
     `WORKFLOW_YAML_LIMIT` = `{extension: "yml", max_lines: 2_000, warn_lines: 1_500, label: "GitHub Actions workflow"}` (cap deliberately above today's worst file so it warns rather than blocks unrelated PRs). Intro string now reads "…Rust, Links Notation, worker JavaScript, and GitHub Actions workflow files…". Two new tests added: `check_directory_measures_github_workflows` and `check_directory_does_not_measure_quoted_case_study_workflows` (uses `docs/case-studies/issue-561/template-comparison/js/release.yml`). Verified: 14 tests pass; release.yml at 1824 lines emits a warning, script exits 0.

   - **`.github/workflows/release.yml`** — five changes this session:
     (a) Clippy now gates:
     ```yaml
     # `-D warnings` is required, not optional: every lint in `[lints.clippy]`
     # of Cargo.toml is set to `warn`, so without it clippy printed findings
     # and still exited 0 -- a green check over unreviewed lint debt
     # (issue #812). The tree is clippy-clean at the time this was added, so
     # this promotes existing behaviour rather than introducing new failures.
     - name: Run Clippy
       run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
     ```
     (b) Two new lint steps before "Check formatting":
     ```yaml
     - name: Lint GitHub Actions workflows
       env:
         ACTIONLINT_VERSION: 1.7.7
       run: |
         set -euo pipefail
         curl -fsSL \
           "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/releases/download/v${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}/actionlint_${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
           | tar xz -C /tmp actionlint
         /tmp/actionlint -color

     - name: Lint shell scripts
       run: |
         set -euo pipefail
         mapfile -t scripts < <(
           git ls-files '*.sh' \
             | grep -v -e '^experiments/' -e '^dev/log/' -e '^docs/case-studies/'
         )
         if [ ${#scripts[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
           echo "::error::No shell scripts matched; the lint selector is broken" >&2
           exit 1
         fi
         printf 'Linting %s shell script(s)\n' "${#scripts[@]}"
         shellcheck --severity=warning "${scripts[@]}"
     ```
     (c) Release gates (both `auto-release` and `manual-release`):
     ```yaml
     needs: [lint, test, build, secrets-scan, test-e2e-local, test-agent-cli-e2e]
     if: |
       !cancelled() &&
       github.event_name == 'push' &&
       github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
       needs.build.result == 'success' &&
       needs.secrets-scan.result != 'failure' &&
       needs.test-e2e-local.result != 'failure' &&
       needs.test-agent-cli-e2e.result != 'failure'
     ```
     (d) "Run desktop library tests" rewritten from `node --test $(ls desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs | grep -v '/web-tools.test.mjs)` to an explicit `shopt -s nullglob` + array build + empty-list `::error` guard + `node --test "${tests[@]}"`.
     (e) `echo "$HOME/.bun/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"` (was unquoted).
     Also carried over uncommitted from before compaction: `include-hidden-files: true` on `upload-pages-artifact`, and screenshot upload changed from `if: always()` to `if-no-files-found: error`.

   - **`.github/actionlint.yaml`** — NEW:
     ```yaml
     self-hosted-runner:
       labels:
         - macos-15-intel
         - windows-11-arm
     ```
     (Both are real 2025 GitHub-hosted runners newer than actionlint 1.7.7's baked-in label list.)

   - **`.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml`** — four changes:
     (a) `finalize` job: `always()` → `!cancelled()` with a comment explaining that `gh release upload --clobber` under `always()` lets a cancelled run overwrite a complete `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial one.
     (b) `build` and `vscode` jobs gained `fetch-depth: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && '0' || '1' }}` plus a "Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)" step running `bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` with `BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}`.
     (c) SC2066 fix: `dmgs=("release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg")` then `for dmg in "${dmgs[@]}"; do`.
     (d) SC2012 fix in vscode packaging: `shopt -s nullglob; vsix_files=(formal-ai-vscode-*.vsix); vsix="${vsix_files[0]:-}"`.

   - **`scripts/check-secrets.sh`** — secretlint pinned:
     ```bash
     SECRETLINT_VERSION="${SECRETLINT_VERSION:-13.0.2}"
     debug "secretlint ${SECRETLINT_VERSION}"
     printf '%s\0' "${files[@]}" |
       xargs -0 npx --yes \
         -p "secretlint@${SECRETLINT_VERSION}" \
         -p "@secretlint/secretlint-rule-preset-recommend@${SECRETLINT_VERSION}" \
         secretlint
     ```

   - **`scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`** — `BEHIND_COUNT=$(git rev-list --count "HEAD..origin/$BASE_REF")` and `if git merge "origin/$BASE_REF" --no-edit; then`; header note changed from "Adopted verbatim" to explain the quoting divergence from upstream.

   - **`scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh`** — healing-build notice added inside `emit_outputs()`:
     ```bash
     if [ "$should_build" = "true" ] && [ -n "$tag" ]; then
       head_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/HEAD" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
       tag_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/${tag}" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
       if [ -n "$head_sha" ] && [ -n "$tag_sha" ] && [ "$head_sha" != "$tag_sha" ]; then
         echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. ..."
       fi
     fi
     ```

   - **`tests/unit/ci-cd/workflow_release.rs`** — two tests added before `release_workflow_jobs_have_explicit_timeouts`: `releases_do_not_publish_past_a_failing_secrets_scan_or_e2e_suite` (asserts both release jobs declare each gate in `needs` and compare `!= 'failure'`, filtering out comment lines) and `lint_job_gates_on_workflow_shell_and_clippy_findings` (asserts `-D warnings`, `actionlint`, `shellcheck --severity=warning`).

   - **`REQUIREMENTS.md`** — R474 amended (artifact/lockfile exclusion, 94.20% of `v0.300.0..main`); R478 amended (Report at release, Enforce+differential at PR gate, named the covering tests); R480 added (METRIC_VERSION epochs).

   - **`changelog.d/20260720_150000_issue_812_ci_false_positives.md`** — NEW, `bump: patch`, with Fixed/Added sections covering all of the above.

4. **Errors and fixes:**
   - `sudo apt-get install -y shellcheck` failed (exit 1) → downloaded the static binary `shellcheck-v0.10.0.linux.x86_64.tar.xz` to `/tmp/shellcheck` instead.
   - A `sleep 60; tail ...` chain was blocked by the harness → replaced with `until grep -q "test result:" <file>; do sleep 5; done`.
   - Two concurrent `cargo test --test unit` background runs deadlocked on the cargo build-directory lock (both reported exit 144 after `pkill -f "cargo test"`) → killed both and started a single run writing to `ci-logs/unit-tests.log` (background ID `b0t3mkm7h`).
   - An early `grep -rn ... .` appeared to show `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` had no caller in this repo's workflows; re-grepping `.github/workflows/` explicitly showed it IS called at release.yml:317 and :540. The tool (ugrep) skips hidden directories by default. The F2 finding was still valid but narrower: only `desktop-release.yml` lacked it.
   - Verified actionlint genuinely type-checks by injecting `needs.secrets-scanX` into a copy — it errored, confirming the real file's expressions are validated.
   - No user feedback or corrections were received at any point (only one user message exists in the entire conversation).

5. **Problem Solving:**

   **Defect A (from before compaction, already committed as `e80d47ee`)** — `Auto Release` deadlocked on the self-hosting ratchet. Two root causes, both fixed and verified: (1) the denominator measured log volume — 599,340 of 636,240 lines (94.20%) in `v0.300.0..main` were captured CI transcripts the repo requires each iteration to commit; fixed by symmetric exclusion of captured artifacts + lockfiles with `METRIC_VERSION = 2` starting a new comparison epoch. (2) enforcement sat where nobody could act (on `main`, after commits were immutable, blocking the only thing that moves the range); fixed with `RatchetPolicy::Report` at release time and a differential `--check-ratchet` PR gate. Verified: the exact failing command now exits 0 → `4.36% (1608/36896 changed lines; 10/119 commits)`.

   **Defect B** — macOS codesign failure is a `workflow_run` healing build of tag v0.300.0, which predates the four merged packaging fixes; the fixed code is proven green (PR run 29746221627: `considered=1479 skipped=751`). No `desktop/` change needed — it needs a newer tag, which Defect A's fix unblocks. This session added the `::notice` so a red healing build is self-explaining rather than re-diagnosed.

   **This session's sweep (all verified locally):** the size gate never saw a workflow (`.git` substring); clippy never gated (no `-D warnings`); releases could publish past a failing secrets scan or E2E suite; the desktop `finalize` job could clobber a complete checksum manifest on cancel; desktop packaging validated a stale merge preview; `node --test $(ls | grep)` could silently run zero tests; secretlint floated to `latest`; `$BASE_REF` was unquoted; nothing linted the pipeline definitions or the shipped shell scripts at all.

   Final verification state: `actionlint -shellcheck=/tmp/shellcheck -pyflakes=` → EXIT=0 across all workflows; `shellcheck --severity=warning` over 17 shipped scripts → EXIT=0; `cargo fmt --all -- --check` → OK; `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` → EXIT=0; `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` → EXIT=0; `ci_cd::check_file_size` 14/14 pass; `ci_cd::desktop_release_resolve` 10/10 pass.

6. **All user messages:**
   - "Issue to solve: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/812 / Your prepared branch: issue-812-7f9674a82402 / Your prepared working directory: /tmp/gh-issue-solver-1784567326843 / Your prepared Pull Request: https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/813 — Download all logs and collect data related about the issue to this repository, and compile that data into the ./dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813 folder. Use the collected evidence to do a deep analysis (search online for additional facts and data), reconstruct the timeline/sequence of events, list each and every requirement from the issue, find the root cause of each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement. Also check online for known existing components/libraries that solve a similar problem or can help. If there is not enough data to find the actual root cause, add debug output and a verbose mode (if not already present) so the root cause can be found on the next iteration. Keep the default state switched off. If the issue is related to another repository/project, report issues on GitHub for that project when possible. Each report must contain reproducible examples, workarounds, and suggestions for fixing the issue in code. Double-check that the requirements are fully applied to the entire codebase: if an issue exists in multiple places, apply it in all of them. Proceed."
   - A final instruction to produce this summary as text only, with no tool calls.
   - (No other user messages. All background-task notifications explicitly state they are NOT user input and must not be treated as approval.)

7. **Pending Tasks:**
   - #1 Collect CI evidence into `dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813` — DONE (committed `e80d47ee`)
   - #2 Fix self-hosting metric denominator — DONE (committed, verified)
   - #3 Move ratchet enforcement to where it is actionable — DONE (committed, verified)
   - #4 Fix macOS desktop codesign failure — root cause established, unblocked by #2/#3; healing-build `::notice` added this session; the optional unverified `overrides: {"@electron/osx-sign": "^2.6.0"}` pin remains undecided
   - #5 Apply CI/CD template sweep findings — essentially complete, UNCOMMITTED
   - #6 Analysis (done), regression tests (done), changelog fragment (done), REQUIREMENTS.md (done); still to do: confirm the full `cargo test --test unit` run, commit + push, update PR 813 description, `gh pr ready 813`
   - Still pending: file the two drafted upstream issues against the python template (no workflow-level `permissions:`; redundant `always() && !cancelled()` at `:105`, `:186`, `:517`), and an upstream report for the `simulate-fresh-merge.sh` unquoted `$BASE_REF` in the js template
   - `dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813/analysis.md` should be updated to record which sweep findings were applied and how

8. **Current Work:**

   I was finishing the second atomic commit (the template/CI sweep). Everything listed in §3 is edited on disk but **nothing from this session is committed yet**. The immediately preceding action was adding the healing-build `::notice` to `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh::emit_outputs()` and verifying it:
   ```
   /tmp/shellcheck --severity=warning scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh; echo "EXIT=$?"  →  EXIT=0
   cargo test --test unit ci_cd::desktop_release_resolve  →  10 passed; 0 failed
   ```
   A full `cargo test --test unit` run is in flight in the background (ID `b0t3mkm7h`, output to `ci-logs/unit-tests.log`) and its result has NOT yet been read. That run is the gate before committing, because the new assertions in `tests/unit/ci-cd/workflow_release.rs` and the `needs:` changes in `release.yml` could interact with existing workflow-structure tests (e.g. `release_workflow_jobs_have_explicit_timeouts`, `change_gated_jobs_never_depend_on_a_skipped_changelog`).

9. **Optional Next Step:**

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Resolve which release tag the Desktop Release workflow should build assets for,
# and whether a build is needed at all.
#
# Extracted from .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml so the decision logic
# (the part that regressed in issue #479) is unit-testable with a mocked `gh`
# CLI. See tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs.
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Background (issue #479: "Not available in latest release" for all desktop apps)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The automated CI/CD release (scripts/version-and-commit.rs) bumps the version
# in a NEW child commit ("chore: release vX.Y.Z"), annotates THAT commit with the
# vX.Y.Z tag, and creates the GitHub release from it -- all pushed with
# GITHUB_TOKEN. GitHub therefore:
#   * suppresses the `release` event for that release (recursion guard), and
#   * never starts a CI run for the child commit (also recursion guard).
# The "CI/CD Pipeline" run that DOES complete carries the PARENT commit's SHA
# (the commit CI actually ran on). The release tag points at the child commit,
# whose first parent IS that head SHA.
#
# The previous resolve logic required a tag whose commit EQUALS the workflow_run
# head SHA. Because the tag lives on the child commit, that match NEVER
# succeeded, so the build job was skipped, no assets were uploaded, and every
# /download entry read "Not available in latest release".
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolution tiers (workflow_run)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Tier 1 (defensive): a tag whose commit IS the head SHA. Future-proof in case
#           the release flow ever stops creating a child commit.
#   Tier 2 (normal):    the latest published release -- the auto-release child
#           commit whose first parent is the head SHA. A diagnostic check
#           confirms the parent relationship and records it in the log, but the
#           build proceeds regardless so the page self-heals.
# An idempotency / self-healing guard then skips the build only when the resolved
# release already carries every expected desktop asset. A partial release (for
# example macOS/Windows present but Linux missing) must rebuild so it self-heals.
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inputs (environment)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   EVENT                  github.event_name (release|workflow_dispatch|workflow_run)
#   INPUT_TAG              workflow_dispatch input tag (optional)
#   RELEASE_TAG            release event tag (github.event.release.tag_name)
#   REPO                   owner/name (required)
#   WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA  head SHA of the completed CI run (workflow_run only)
#   GH_TOKEN               token for the gh CLI
#   GITHUB_OUTPUT          file to append `tag=`/`should_build=` (optional; the
#                          script also always prints the resolved values so local
#                          runs and tests can read them from stdout)
set -euo pipefail

EVENT="${EVENT:-}"
INPUT_TAG="${INPUT_TAG:-}"
RELEASE_TAG="${RELEASE_TAG:-}"
REPO="${REPO:?REPO is required}"
WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA="${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA:-}"

tag=""
should_build=true
resolution="default"

group() { echo "::group::$*"; }
endgroup() { echo "::endgroup::"; }
log() { echo "[desktop-release-resolve] $*"; }

emit_outputs() {
  log "result: tag='${tag}' should_build='${should_build}' resolution='${resolution}'"
  # Issue #812: a healing build checks out the *tag*, so it packages that tag's
  # code -- not `main`. Run 29752745259 failed macOS signing on v0.300.0, which
  # predates the four packaging fixes already merged, and the red run read as
  # "desktop packaging is broken" when it meant "the last tag is old". Say so in
  # the run summary rather than leaving it to be re-diagnosed each time.
  if [ "$should_build" = "true" ] && [ -n "$tag" ]; then
    head_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/HEAD" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    tag_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/${tag}" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [ -n "$head_sha" ] && [ -n "$tag_sha" ] && [ "$head_sha" != "$tag_sha" ]; then
      echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. This run builds that tag's code; fixes merged after it are not included, so a failure here does not necessarily mean current code is broken."
    fi
  fi
  if [ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]; then
    {
      echo "tag=$tag"
      echo "should_build=$should_build"
    } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  fi
}

latest_release_tag() {
  gh release view --repo "$REPO" --json tagName --jq .tagName 2>/dev/null || true
}

release_version_from_tag() {
  local value="$1"
  if [[ "$value" =~ (^|-)v?([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+([-+][0-9A-Za-z.-]+)?) ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
  fi
}

expected_desktop_assets() {
  local version="$1"
  printf '%s\n' \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-arm64-${version}.dmg" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-arm64-${version}.zip" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-x64-${version}.dmg" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-x64-${version}.zip" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-installer-x64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-installer-arm64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-portable-x64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-portable-arm64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.AppImage" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.AppImage" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.deb" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.deb" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.tar.gz" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.tar.gz" \
    "latest.yml" \
    "latest-mac.yml" \
    "latest-linux.yml"
}

group "desktop-release resolve inputs"
log "event                 = ${EVENT:-<none>}"
log "input_tag             = ${INPUT_TAG:-<none>}"
log "release_tag           = ${RELEASE_TAG:-<none>}"
log "repo                  = ${REPO}"
log "workflow_run_head_sha = ${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA:-<none>}"
endgroup

case "$EVENT" in
  release)
    tag="$RELEASE_TAG"
    resolution="release-event"
    ;;
  workflow_dispatch)
    tag="${INPUT_TAG:-}"
    resolution="workflow_dispatch-input"
    ;;
  workflow_run)
    if [ -z "$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA" ]; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-missing-head-sha"
      log "workflow_run payload carried no head SHA; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi

    # Tier 1 (defensive): a tag whose commit IS the completed CI head SHA.
    group "Tier 1: exact tag on head SHA ${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA}"
    exact="$(gh api "repos/$REPO/tags?per_page=100" --paginate \
      --jq ".[] | select(.commit.sha == \"$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA\") | .name" 2>/dev/null \
      | grep -E '^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -n 1 || true)"
    log "exact-match tag: ${exact:-<none>}"
    endgroup

    if [ -n "$exact" ]; then
      tag="$exact"
      resolution="workflow_run-exact-sha"
    else
      # Tier 2 (normal): the auto-release tags a CHILD "chore: release vX.Y.Z"
      # commit whose first parent is this head SHA, so no tag points at the head
      # SHA directly. Resolve the latest published release -- that child release.
      group "Tier 2: latest published release (auto-release child commit)"
      tag="$(latest_release_tag)"
      log "latest release tag: ${tag:-<none>}"
      if [ -n "$tag" ]; then
        # Diagnostic only: confirm the latest release descends from this CI run.
        # `gh api .../commits/<tag>` dereferences the annotated tag to its commit.
        parent="$(gh api "repos/$REPO/commits/$tag" --jq '.parents[0].sha' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [ -n "$parent" ] && [ "$parent" = "$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA" ]; then
          log "confirmed: ${tag} commit parent is the CI head SHA (auto-release child)."
          resolution="workflow_run-child-of-head"
        else
          log "note: ${tag} commit parent='${parent:-<none>}' != head SHA; using latest release as self-healing fallback."
          resolution="workflow_run-latest-fallback"
        fi
      fi
      endgroup
    fi

    if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-no-release"
      log "No published release found; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi

    if ! gh release view "$tag" --repo "$REPO" --json tagName >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-release-missing"
      log "No GitHub release exists for resolved tag ${tag}; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi
    ;;
esac

# release / workflow_dispatch with an empty tag fall back to the latest release.
if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
  tag="$(latest_release_tag)"
  resolution="${resolution}+latest"
fi

if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
  log "Could not resolve any release tag to build; skipping." >&2
  should_build=false
  emit_outputs
  exit 0
fi

log "Resolved release tag: ${tag}"

# Idempotency / self-healing guard for automatic (workflow_run) builds: only
# skip when the resolved release already has the complete expected desktop
# artifact set. This:
#   * avoids rebuilding complete assets that already exist (pipeline re-runs, or
#     runs that did not cut a new release and fall back to the latest one),
#   * self-heals the original empty-asset backlog, and
#   * self-heals partial releases such as v0.204.0, where macOS/Windows assets
#     existed but Linux artifacts were still missing.
# Manual `release`/`workflow_dispatch` runs intentionally rebuild (clobber) so a
# maintainer can force a refresh.
group "Idempotency guard: required desktop assets on ${tag}"
release_version="$(release_version_from_tag "$tag" || true)"
if [ -z "$release_version" ]; then
  log "Could not parse semver from ${tag}; leaving build enabled rather than risking a silent skip."
else
  # Keep the query's exit status: a failed `gh` and a release with genuinely no
  # assets both yield an empty list, but only one of them is a problem worth
  # naming in the log. Either way the fail-safe direction is the same (build),
  # so the status only drives diagnostics, never the decision.
  if existing_names="$(gh release view "$tag" --repo "$REPO" --json assets \
    --jq '.assets[].name | select(startswith("formal-ai-desktop-") or . == "latest.yml" or . == "latest-mac.yml" or . == "latest-linux.yml")' 2>/dev/null)"; then
    :
  else
    log "warning: could not list assets for ${tag} (gh exited non-zero); treating them as absent and building."
    existing_names=""
  fi
  existing_count="$(printf '%s\n' "$existing_names" | sed '/^$/d' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
  missing=()
  while IFS= read -r expected; do
    if ! printf '%s\n' "$existing_names" | grep -Fxq "$expected"; then
      missing+=("$expected")
    fi
  done < <(expected_desktop_assets "$release_version")

  log "release version: ${release_version}"
  log "existing desktop assets: ${existing_count}"
  log "required desktop assets: 17"
  if [ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    log "all required desktop assets are present."
  else
    log "missing required desktop assets (${#missing[@]}):"
    printf '  %s\n' "${missing[@]}"
  fi
fi
endgroup
if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_run" ] && [ -n "${release_version:-}" ] && [ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  should_build=false
  resolution="${resolution}+already-has-all-assets"
  log "Release ${tag} already has the complete desktop asset set; skipping automatic build."
fi

emit_outputs

---
bump: patch
---

### Fixed
- Stopped the self-hosting ratchet from deadlocking every release: the metric no longer counts captured CI transcripts (`*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.diff`, `*.patch`, `*.stderr`, `*.stdout`) or dependency lockfiles, which made up 94.20% of the measured range and turned "commit your evidence" into a guaranteed regression, and enforcement moved from `record_release` on `main` — where every contributing commit is already immutable — to a differential pull-request gate where the commits can still be amended (issue #812).
- `cargo clippy` now runs with `-D warnings`. Every lint in `[lints.clippy]` is set to `warn`, so the job printed findings and still exited 0 (issue #812).
- `auto-release` and `manual-release` now gate on `Secrets Scan` and both E2E suites, not just `[lint, test, build]`; a red secrets scan on `main` could previously publish the crate, the Docker image and the GitHub Release anyway (issue #812).
- The desktop `finalize` job runs under `!cancelled()` instead of `always()`, so a cancelled run can no longer clobber a complete `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial one via `gh release upload --clobber` (issue #812).
- The desktop packaging and VS Code jobs now simulate the fresh merge on pull requests, the same way `lint` and `test` already did, so packaging is validated against the merge result rather than a stale merge preview (issue #812).
- `scripts/check-file-size.rs` excluded `.github/workflows/**` by accident: its `.git` exclusion was matched as a substring. Directory exclusions are now matched per path component, GitHub Actions workflows are measured (warn at 1500 lines, fail at 2000), and quoted third-party workflows under `docs/case-studies/**` stay exempt (issue #812).
- `node --test $(ls … | grep -v …)` in the desktop library test step fell back to Node's own test discovery if the glob ever stopped matching — a green step running none of the intended tests. The file list is now built explicitly and an empty list fails (issue #812).
- Pinned `secretlint` to an explicit version in `scripts/check-secrets.sh`; `npx --yes -p secretlint` resolved to whatever `latest` was when the job ran (issue #812).
- Quoted `$BASE_REF` at every expansion in `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, which upstream leaves bare on two of three lines (issue #812).

### Added
- `actionlint` (with `shellcheck`) now lints every workflow definition, and `shellcheck` lints the shipped `*.sh` scripts. Nothing validated either before, so a mistyped `needs.<job>` reference or a malformed expression — which fails *open*, silently skipping the guarded step under a green check — could only be found by pushing (issue #812).
- `METRIC_VERSION` on self-hosting ledger rows, so a change to how the share is measured starts a new comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history; rows from different epochs are never compared (issue #812).

git add -A && git commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
fix(ci): close the false-positive and false-negative gaps across the pipeline

Issue #812 asks for every false positive, false negative, warning and
error in CI/CD. The second class -- checks that pass without checking --
turned out to be the larger one. Each item below was reproduced locally
before it was fixed.

Checks that never gated:

* `cargo clippy` ran without `-D warnings`. Every lint in `[lints.clippy]`
  is `warn`, so the job printed findings and exited 0. The tree is clean
  today, so this promotes existing behaviour rather than adding failures.
* `scripts/check-file-size.rs` matched its directory exclusions as
  substrings, so `.git` hid `.github/workflows/**` from the size gate
  entirely. Exclusions are now per path component, and workflows are
  measured (warn 1500, fail 2000) while third-party workflows quoted
  under `docs/case-studies/**` stay exempt.
* Nothing validated the workflow definitions or the shipped shell
  scripts. A mistyped `needs.<job>` reference fails *open* -- the guarded
  step silently skips under a green check. actionlint (with shellcheck)
  and shellcheck now run in `lint`.
* `node --test $(ls … | grep -v …)` fell back to Node's own discovery if
  the glob stopped matching, i.e. a green step running none of the
  intended tests. The list is built explicitly and an empty list errors.

Checks that gated the wrong thing:

* `auto-release`/`manual-release` needed only `[lint, test, build]`, so a
  red `Secrets Scan` or E2E suite on `main` still published the crate,
  the Docker image and the GitHub Release.
* The desktop `finalize` job ran under `always()`, letting a cancelled
  run clobber a complete `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial one.
* Desktop packaging and VS Code jobs validated a stale merge preview;
  they now simulate the fresh merge as `lint`/`test` already did.
* `secretlint` floated to whatever `latest` was at run time; pinned.
* `$BASE_REF` was unquoted in `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh`.

A healing build of an existing tag now says so with a `::notice`: run
29752745259 failed macOS signing on v0.300.0, which predates the four
merged packaging fixes, and read as "packaging is broken" when it meant
"the last tag is old".

Verified: actionlint over all workflows exits 0; shellcheck
--severity=warning over the 17 shipped scripts exits 0; cargo fmt,
cargo clippy -D warnings and check-file-size.rs exit 0; the full unit
suite passes 1953/1953 with four new regression tests.

Formal-AI-Session: issue-812-claude-20260720
Formal-AI-Evidence: dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813/analysis.md
EOF
git log -1 --stat --format='%h %s'

254 dev/log/issues/812/pulls/813/analysis.md
| # | Class | Finding |
| --- | --- | --- |
| F3 | false negative | `auto-release` needs only `[lint, test, build]`; `test-e2e-local`, `test-agent-cli-e2e`, `secrets-scan` and `docker-build` can all be red while a crate and a GitHub release publish from `main`. |
| F1 | false negative | The file-size gate covers no workflow YAML and no `.md`; `release.yml` is 1794 lines against the js template's 1500 ceiling, and CI is green. |
| F2 | false negative | `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` exists but is invoked nowhere in `desktop-release.yml`, so the dry run builds the PR head rather than the merge result. |
| F4 | false positive risk | `desktop-release.yml` `finalize` uses `always()`: a *cancelled* run still `--clobber`s `SHA256SUMS.txt` with a partial manifest. |
| F13 | silent no-op | `FILE_SIZE_WARNING_BASE` is empty on `workflow_dispatch` and first push; the rust template has a `git ls-files` fallback. |
| F5 | silent data loss | `upload-pages-artifact` lacks `include-hidden-files: true`, so dotfiles are dropped from the Pages tarball. |
| F6/F7 | silent no-op | Screenshot and desktop checksum uploads are `if: always()` with the default `if-no-files-found: warn`. |
| F12 | supply chain | `npx --yes secretlint` is unpinned, and the scan is diff-scoped where the rust template scans the full tree. |
| F10 | shell | `scripts/install.sh:30` uses `set -eu` without `pipefail` — the only script in the repo missing it. |

Verified negatives, recorded so they are not re-investigated: all 21 jobs carry
`timeout-minutes`, there is no `continue-on-error` anywhere, both workflows set
top-level `permissions: contents: read`, and every `scripts/*.sh` except
`install.sh` sets `set -euo pipefail`.

One further false negative found outside the sweep: `cargo clippy` runs
**without** `-D warnings` (`release.yml:355`), so lint warnings can never fail
the build. Every warning this change would have introduced was fixed rather than
tolerated.

## 7. Upstream reports

Two defects reproduce in the templates themselves and are drafted for filing in
`analysis/template-sweep.md`:

- python template `release.yml` has no workflow-level `permissions:` block;
- python template uses the redundant `always() && !cancelled()` at `:105`, `:186`
  and `:517`.

## 8. Debug output

Kept off by default, per the issue instructions:

- `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG=1` — per-path `[adhoc-sign-mac]` decisions from the
  sign hook (added in `1b1586bb`; its *absence* from the failing log is what
  proved finding §5.3).
- `experiments/self_hosting_ratchet_replay/replay.py` — per-commit attribution,
  reason and subject for any range, without `rust-script`.
