#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Resolve which release tag the Desktop Release workflow should build assets for,
# and whether a build is needed at all.
#
# Extracted from .github/workflows/desktop-release.yml so the decision logic
# (the part that regressed in issue #479) is unit-testable with a mocked `gh`
# CLI. See tests/unit/ci-cd/desktop_release_resolve.rs.
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Background (issue #479: "Not available in latest release" for all desktop apps)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# The automated CI/CD release (scripts/version-and-commit.rs) bumps the version
# in a NEW child commit ("chore: release vX.Y.Z"), annotates THAT commit with the
# vX.Y.Z tag, and creates the GitHub release from it -- all pushed with
# GITHUB_TOKEN. GitHub therefore:
#   * suppresses the `release` event for that release (recursion guard), and
#   * never starts a CI run for the child commit (also recursion guard).
# The "CI/CD Pipeline" run that DOES complete carries the PARENT commit's SHA
# (the commit CI actually ran on). The release tag points at the child commit,
# whose first parent IS that head SHA.
#
# The previous resolve logic required a tag whose commit EQUALS the workflow_run
# head SHA. Because the tag lives on the child commit, that match NEVER
# succeeded, so the build job was skipped, no assets were uploaded, and every
# /download entry read "Not available in latest release".
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Resolution tiers (workflow_run)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   Tier 1 (defensive): a tag whose commit IS the head SHA. Future-proof in case
#           the release flow ever stops creating a child commit.
#   Tier 2 (normal):    the latest published release -- the auto-release child
#           commit whose first parent is the head SHA. A diagnostic check
#           confirms the parent relationship and records it in the log, but the
#           build proceeds regardless so the page self-heals.
# An idempotency / self-healing guard then skips the build only when the resolved
# release already carries every expected desktop asset. A partial release (for
# example macOS/Windows present but Linux missing) must rebuild so it self-heals.
#
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inputs (environment)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
#   EVENT                  github.event_name (release|workflow_dispatch|workflow_run)
#   INPUT_TAG              workflow_dispatch input tag (optional)
#   RELEASE_TAG            release event tag (github.event.release.tag_name)
#   REPO                   owner/name (required)
#   WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA  head SHA of the completed CI run (workflow_run only)
#   GH_TOKEN               token for the gh CLI
#   GITHUB_OUTPUT          file to append `tag=`/`should_build=` (optional; the
#                          script also always prints the resolved values so local
#                          runs and tests can read them from stdout)
set -euo pipefail

EVENT="${EVENT:-}"
INPUT_TAG="${INPUT_TAG:-}"
RELEASE_TAG="${RELEASE_TAG:-}"
REPO="${REPO:?REPO is required}"
WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA="${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA:-}"

tag=""
should_build=true
resolution="default"

group() { echo "::group::$*"; }
endgroup() { echo "::endgroup::"; }
log() { echo "[desktop-release-resolve] $*"; }

emit_outputs() {
  log "result: tag='${tag}' should_build='${should_build}' resolution='${resolution}'"
  # Issue #812: a healing build checks out the *tag*, so it packages that tag's
  # code -- not `main`. Run 29752745259 failed macOS signing on v0.300.0, which
  # predates the four packaging fixes already merged, and the red run read as
  # "desktop packaging is broken" when it meant "the last tag is old". Say so in
  # the run summary rather than leaving it to be re-diagnosed each time.
  if [ "$should_build" = "true" ] && [ -n "$tag" ]; then
    head_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/HEAD" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    tag_sha="$(gh api "repos/${REPO}/commits/${tag}" --jq .sha 2>/dev/null || echo "")"
    if [ -n "$head_sha" ] && [ -n "$tag_sha" ] && [ "$head_sha" != "$tag_sha" ]; then
      echo "::notice title=Healing build of an existing tag::Packaging ${tag}, which is not the tip of the default branch. This run builds that tag's code; fixes merged after it are not included, so a failure here does not necessarily mean current code is broken."
    fi
  fi
  if [ -n "${GITHUB_OUTPUT:-}" ]; then
    {
      echo "tag=$tag"
      echo "should_build=$should_build"
    } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
  fi
}

latest_release_tag() {
  gh release view --repo "$REPO" --json tagName --jq .tagName 2>/dev/null || true
}

release_version_from_tag() {
  local value="$1"
  if [[ "$value" =~ (^|-)v?([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+([-+][0-9A-Za-z.-]+)?) ]]; then
    printf '%s\n' "${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
  fi
}

expected_desktop_assets() {
  local version="$1"
  printf '%s\n' \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-arm64-${version}.dmg" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-arm64-${version}.zip" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-x64-${version}.dmg" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-macos-x64-${version}.zip" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-installer-x64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-installer-arm64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-portable-x64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-windows-portable-arm64-${version}.exe" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.AppImage" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.AppImage" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.deb" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.deb" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-x64-${version}.tar.gz" \
    "formal-ai-desktop-linux-arm64-${version}.tar.gz" \
    "latest.yml" \
    "latest-mac.yml" \
    "latest-linux.yml"
}

group "desktop-release resolve inputs"
log "event                 = ${EVENT:-<none>}"
log "input_tag             = ${INPUT_TAG:-<none>}"
log "release_tag           = ${RELEASE_TAG:-<none>}"
log "repo                  = ${REPO}"
log "workflow_run_head_sha = ${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA:-<none>}"
endgroup

case "$EVENT" in
  release)
    tag="$RELEASE_TAG"
    resolution="release-event"
    ;;
  workflow_dispatch)
    tag="${INPUT_TAG:-}"
    resolution="workflow_dispatch-input"
    ;;
  workflow_run)
    if [ -z "$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA" ]; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-missing-head-sha"
      log "workflow_run payload carried no head SHA; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi

    # Tier 1 (defensive): a tag whose commit IS the completed CI head SHA.
    group "Tier 1: exact tag on head SHA ${WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA}"
    exact="$(gh api "repos/$REPO/tags?per_page=100" --paginate \
      --jq ".[] | select(.commit.sha == \"$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA\") | .name" 2>/dev/null \
      | grep -E '^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -n 1 || true)"
    log "exact-match tag: ${exact:-<none>}"
    endgroup

    if [ -n "$exact" ]; then
      tag="$exact"
      resolution="workflow_run-exact-sha"
    else
      # Tier 2 (normal): the auto-release tags a CHILD "chore: release vX.Y.Z"
      # commit whose first parent is this head SHA, so no tag points at the head
      # SHA directly. Resolve the latest published release -- that child release.
      group "Tier 2: latest published release (auto-release child commit)"
      tag="$(latest_release_tag)"
      log "latest release tag: ${tag:-<none>}"
      if [ -n "$tag" ]; then
        # Diagnostic only: confirm the latest release descends from this CI run.
        # `gh api .../commits/<tag>` dereferences the annotated tag to its commit.
        parent="$(gh api "repos/$REPO/commits/$tag" --jq '.parents[0].sha' 2>/dev/null || true)"
        if [ -n "$parent" ] && [ "$parent" = "$WORKFLOW_RUN_HEAD_SHA" ]; then
          log "confirmed: ${tag} commit parent is the CI head SHA (auto-release child)."
          resolution="workflow_run-child-of-head"
        else
          log "note: ${tag} commit parent='${parent:-<none>}' != head SHA; using latest release as self-healing fallback."
          resolution="workflow_run-latest-fallback"
        fi
      fi
      endgroup
    fi

    if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-no-release"
      log "No published release found; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi

    if ! gh release view "$tag" --repo "$REPO" --json tagName >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      should_build=false
      resolution="workflow_run-release-missing"
      log "No GitHub release exists for resolved tag ${tag}; skipping desktop build."
      emit_outputs
      exit 0
    fi
    ;;
esac

# release / workflow_dispatch with an empty tag fall back to the latest release.
if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
  tag="$(latest_release_tag)"
  resolution="${resolution}+latest"
fi

if [ -z "$tag" ]; then
  log "Could not resolve any release tag to build; skipping." >&2
  should_build=false
  emit_outputs
  exit 0
fi

log "Resolved release tag: ${tag}"

# Idempotency / self-healing guard for automatic (workflow_run) builds: only
# skip when the resolved release already has the complete expected desktop
# artifact set. This:
#   * avoids rebuilding complete assets that already exist (pipeline re-runs, or
#     runs that did not cut a new release and fall back to the latest one),
#   * self-heals the original empty-asset backlog, and
#   * self-heals partial releases such as v0.204.0, where macOS/Windows assets
#     existed but Linux artifacts were still missing.
# Manual `release`/`workflow_dispatch` runs intentionally rebuild (clobber) so a
# maintainer can force a refresh.
group "Idempotency guard: required desktop assets on ${tag}"
release_version="$(release_version_from_tag "$tag" || true)"
if [ -z "$release_version" ]; then
  log "Could not parse semver from ${tag}; leaving build enabled rather than risking a silent skip."
else
  # Keep the query's exit status: a failed `gh` and a release with genuinely no
  # assets both yield an empty list, but only one of them is a problem worth
  # naming in the log. Either way the fail-safe direction is the same (build),
  # so the status only drives diagnostics, never the decision.
  if existing_names="$(gh release view "$tag" --repo "$REPO" --json assets \
    --jq '.assets[].name | select(startswith("formal-ai-desktop-") or . == "latest.yml" or . == "latest-mac.yml" or . == "latest-linux.yml")' 2>/dev/null)"; then
    :
  else
    log "warning: could not list assets for ${tag} (gh exited non-zero); treating them as absent and building."
    existing_names=""
  fi
  existing_count="$(printf '%s\n' "$existing_names" | sed '/^$/d' | wc -l | tr -d ' ')"
  missing=()
  while IFS= read -r expected; do
    if ! printf '%s\n' "$existing_names" | grep -Fxq "$expected"; then
      missing+=("$expected")
    fi
  done < <(expected_desktop_assets "$release_version")

  log "release version: ${release_version}"
  log "existing desktop assets: ${existing_count}"
  log "required desktop assets: 17"
  if [ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    log "all required desktop assets are present."
  else
    log "missing required desktop assets (${#missing[@]}):"
    printf '  %s\n' "${missing[@]}"
  fi
fi
endgroup
if [ "$EVENT" = "workflow_run" ] && [ -n "${release_version:-}" ] && [ ${#missing[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
  should_build=false
  resolution="${resolution}+already-has-all-assets"
  log "Release ${tag} already has the complete desktop asset set; skipping automatic build."
fi

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

content.content:
**1. `Auto Release` deadlocked on the self-hosting ratchet.** Two independent root causes:

- *The denominator measured log volume, not authorship.* 599,340 of the 636,240 lines in `v0.300.0..main` — **94.20%** — were captured CI transcripts and lockfiles that this repository *requires* each iteration to commit. Committing the evidence the process demands was itself scored as a regression, so the ratchet could only ever tighten past reach. Artifacts (`*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.diff`, `*.patch`, `*.stderr`, `*.stdout`) and lockfiles are now excluded symmetrically from both sides of the ratio, under a new `METRIC_VERSION = 2` so the change starts a fresh comparison epoch instead of silently invalidating recorded history.
- *Enforcement sat where nobody could act on it.* It ran in `record_release`, on `main`, after every contributing commit was already immutable — and it blocked the only mechanism that advances the range. Release-time is now `RatchetPolicy::Report`; enforcement moved to a differential (branch-vs-base) pull-request gate, where the commits can still be amended.

Verified: the exact command that failed in CI now exits 0 — `4.36% (1608/36896 changed lines; 10/119 commits)`.

**2. macOS desktop codesigning.** Not a packaging defect. Run 29752745259 is a `workflow_run` *healing* build of tag `v0.300.0`, which predates the four packaging fixes already merged; the fixed code is proven green in PR run 29746221627 (`considered=1479 skipped=751`). It needs a newer tag, which fix 1 unblocks. Rather than leave this to be re-diagnosed every time, `scripts/desktop-release-resolve.sh` now emits a `::notice` when it is packaging a tag that is not the tip of the default branch.

## Checks that never gated

| What | Why it was green |
| --- | --- |
| `cargo clippy` | Ran without `-D warnings`. Every lint in `[lints.clippy]` is `warn`, so the job printed findings and exited 0. The tree is clean today, so adding it promotes existing behaviour rather than introducing failures. |
| `scripts/check-file-size.rs` | Matched directory exclusions as **substrings**, so `.git` hid all of `.github/workflows/**`. Exclusions are now matched per path component; workflows are measured (warn 1500, fail 2000), while third-party workflows quoted under `docs/case-studies/**` stay exempt — reformatting them would destroy the thing they document. |
| workflow definitions and shell scripts | Nothing validated either. A mistyped `needs.<job>` reference fails **open**: the guarded step silently skips under a green check. `actionlint` 1.7.7 (shellcheck-embedded) and `shellcheck --severity=warning` now run in `lint`. |
| desktop library tests | `node --test $(ls … \| grep -v …)` fell back to Node's own discovery whenever the glob stopped matching — a green step running none of the intended tests. The list is built explicitly and an empty list is a hard error. |

## Checks that gated the wrong thing

- `auto-release`/`manual-release` needed only `[lint, test, build]`. A red **`Secrets Scan`** or either E2E suite on `main` still published the crate, the Docker image and the GitHub Release. (`docker-build` is deliberately *not* a gate: it publishes independently and its failure must not block the crate.)
- The desktop `finalize` job ran under `always()`, so a **cancelled** run could `gh release upload --clobber` a partial `SHA256SUMS.txt` over a complete one. Now `!cancelled()`.
- Desktop packaging and VS Code jobs validated a stale merge preview; they now simulate the fresh merge, as `lint`/`test` already did.
- `secretlint` floated to whatever `latest` was at run time. Pinned.
- `$BASE_REF` was unquoted in `scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh` — a word-split there takes the *skip* branch and reports success without merging.
- `upload-pages-artifact` dropped dotfiles (`include-hidden-files: true`); the screenshot upload was `if: always()` with the default `if-no-files-found: warn`, i.e. green on an empty artifact.

One finding was deliberately **not** "fixed": `scripts/install.sh` is the only script without `pipefail`, because it is the only one running under `#!/usr/bin/env sh`. `pipefail` is not POSIX and aborts dash — the `/bin/sh` a `curl … | sh` install lands in. The rationale is now recorded in the file header so it is not "fixed" later.

## Upstream reports

Three defects reproduce in the templates themselves; all were re-verified against upstream `main` via the API before reporting.

- link-foundation/js-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#111 — **filed in this iteration**: unquoted `$BASE_REF` in `simulate-fresh-merge.sh`, with a reproduction, a workaround and a suggested fix. (The rust template's copy quotes correctly; python does not ship the script.)
- link-foundation/python-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template#33 and #35 — already open upstream, so recorded rather than re-filed.

## Verification

- `cargo test --test unit` → **1953 passed, 0 failed** (6 new regression tests)
- `actionlint -shellcheck` over every workflow → exit 0 (type-checking confirmed live by injecting a bogus `needs.secrets-scanX` into a copy and observing the error)
- `shellcheck --severity=warning` over the 17 shipped scripts → exit 0
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`, `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` → exit 0

New tests: `releases_do_not_publish_past_a_failing_secrets_scan_or_e2e_suite`, `lint_job_gates_on_workflow_shell_and_clippy_findings`, `check_directory_measures_github_workflows`, `check_directory_does_not_measure_quoted_case_study_workflows`, plus the ratchet exclusion and differential-gate tests.

## Debug output (default off, per the issue)

- `FORMAL_AI_MACOS_SIGN_DEBUG=1` — per-path `[adhoc-sign-mac]` sign decisions; its *absence* from the failing log is what proved the healing-build diagnosis.
- `experiments/self_hosting_ratchet_replay/replay.py` — per-commit attribution, reason and subject for any range, without `rust-script`.

## Second pass: two defects found by running the fixed pipeline

Run [29767811026](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29767811026) was the first full run of the repaired pipeline, and it found two more — one introduced by this PR's own first commit.

**`Test (ubuntu-latest)` reported failure with 1953 tests passed and 0 failed.** The suite finished at 18:43:50; `timeout-minutes: 15` killed the job 1.1 seconds later, during teardown. Run 29749095334 on `main` had already done exactly this and was never diagnosed, because GitHub renders a timeout as `cancelled` — indistinguishable from an infrastructure blip. The budget was not padding: checkout plus the two data checks cost ~3m15s before a test runs, the suite takes ~11m, and eight individual agentic/driver tests each exceed 60 s. Recent `main` durations show creep rather than a one-off — 11m02s, 12m27s, 11m49s, 12m57s, **15m16s (cancelled)**, 14m13s. The budget now matches measured cost, and the step emits a `::warning` past 70% of it so the margin cannot be eaten again in silence. Making those eight tests cheaper is separate work with its own regression risk; what is fixed here is the false report.

**The release gates I added would have accepted a timed-out job.** They read `needs.<job>.result != 'failure'`, chosen so a legitimately *skipped* job would not block a release — but the finding above is the proof that a timeout resolves to `'cancelled'`, which is also `!= 'failure'`. Both gates now enumerate the two acceptable results, and the regression test asserts the positive form *and* that the weaker spelling is absent.

That run also independently confirmed the rest: `Self-Hosting Evidence Check` green **on the real PR**, every new lint step green, and [Desktop Release 29767811373](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/29767811373) green across all six platform legs including both macOS architectures — the confirmation that current desktop code signs and packages fine, and that run 29752745259 failed only because it was a healing build of the older `v0.300.0` tag.

old="## Second pass: two defects found by running the fixed pipeline"

diff --git a/.github/actionlint.yaml b/.github/actionlint.yaml
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..a5fc160a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/actionlint.yaml
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# actionlint configuration.
+#
+# `macos-15-intel` and `windows-11-arm` are real GitHub-hosted runner labels
+# (both became generally available in 2025) but are newer than the label list
+# baked into actionlint's release binary, which reports them as unknown. They
+# are declared here so the workflow linter stays free of known-false findings --
+# a linter with permanent noise is a linter nobody reads (issue #812).
+self-hosted-runner:
+  labels:
+    - macos-15-intel
+    - windows-11-arm
diff --git a/.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml b/.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml
index 6e69d959..2fc6a3f5 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/desktop-release.yml
@@ -171,6 +171,23 @@ jobs:
           # On pull requests there is no resolved tag: build the PR head, which
           # is the whole point of the dry run.
           ref: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.tag || '' }}
+          # Quoted '0'/'1': GitHub's `&&`/`||` treat the number 0 as falsy, so an
+          # unquoted 0 here would silently select the other branch.
+          # 0 is needed by simulate-fresh-merge.sh, which merges origin/<base>.
+          fetch-depth: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && '0' || '1' }}
+
+      # Issue #812: `release.yml` simulates the fresh merge in `lint` and `test`,
+      # but this workflow -- the only one that packages the desktop app -- did
+      # not, so the packaging dry run validated the PR head against a merge
+      # preview that predates any commit pushed to the base branch since the last
+      # sync. A packaging break introduced by a concurrent main commit stayed
+      # invisible until after the merge, which is how desktop breakages have
+      # reached tags before.
+      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
+        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
+        env:
+          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
+        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
 
       - name: Install Rust toolchain
         uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
@@ -311,7 +328,11 @@ jobs:
             fi
           done
 
-          for dmg in "release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg"; do
+          # An array, not a bare quoted word: `for x in "a"` reads as an attempt
+          # to word-split and is flagged by shellcheck (SC2066). Behaviour is
+          # unchanged -- exactly one DMG per matrix arch.
+          dmgs=("release/formal-ai-desktop-macos-${ARCH}-${VERSION}.dmg")
+          for dmg in "${dmgs[@]}"; do
             echo "Mounting DMG: $dmg"
             mount_dir="$(mktemp -d)"
             installed_app="$RUNNER_TEMP/formal-ai Desktop.app"
@@ -516,6 +537,15 @@ jobs:
           # On pull requests there is no resolved tag: build the PR head, which
           # is the whole point of the dry run.
           ref: ${{ github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.tag || '' }}
+          # See the note in `build`: quoted so the number 0 is not read as falsy.
+          fetch-depth: ${{ github.event_name == 'pull_request' && '0' || '1' }}
+
+      # Same reason as `build`: package the merge result, not a stale preview.
+      - name: Simulate fresh merge with base branch (PR only)
+        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
+        env:
+          BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
+        run: bash scripts/simulate-fresh-merge.sh
 
       - name: Setup Node
         uses: actions/setup-node@v6
@@ -538,7 +568,11 @@ jobs:
           # filename (formal-ai-vscode-<version>.vsix) matches the release
           # version the /download and /vscode pages resolve.
           npm run package
-          vsix="$(ls formal-ai-vscode-*.vsix 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"
+          # A glob, not `ls | head`: `ls` output is not safe to parse and its
+          # exit status is swallowed here (shellcheck SC2012).
+          shopt -s nullglob
+          vsix_files=(formal-ai-vscode-*.vsix)
+          vsix="${vsix_files[0]:-}"
           [ -n "$vsix" ] || { echo "::error::vsce package produced no .vsix" >&2; exit 1; }
           echo "vsix=$vsix" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
           echo "Packaged: $vsix"
@@ -585,10 +619,15 @@ jobs:
   finalize:
     name: Publish SHA256SUMS.txt + provenance
     needs: [resolve, build, vscode]
-    # always() so a partial matrix (one arch failing under fail-fast:false) still
-    # publishes checksums for the arches that did build.
+    # !cancelled(), not always(): a partial matrix (one arch failing under
+    # fail-fast:false) must still publish checksums for the arches that did
+    # build, but a *cancelled* run must not. The upload below uses
+    # `gh release upload --clobber`, so under always() a run cancelled part-way
+    # -- by the concurrency group superseding it, or by hand -- would overwrite a
+    # complete SHA256SUMS.txt from an earlier successful run with a manifest
+    # covering whichever one or two arches happened to finish (issue #812).
     # Never on pull requests: there is no release to publish checksums to.
-    if: ${{ always() && github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true' }}
+    if: ${{ !cancelled() && github.event_name != 'pull_request' && needs.resolve.outputs.should_build == 'true' }}
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 10
     permissions:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/release.yml b/.github/workflows/release.yml

+++ b/.github/workflows/release.yml
@@ -147,6 +147,22 @@ jobs:
           echo "Validating commits in ${merge_base}..HEAD"
           rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --since "$merge_base" --until HEAD
 
+      # Issue #812: `Auto Release` died on main with "self-hosting ratchet would
+      # fall from 32.68% to 18.24% for v0.301.0" -- after every contributing
+      # commit was already immutable, and while the only way to move the measured
+      # range forward (cutting a tag) was what it blocked. Enforcement belongs
+      # here, where the commits can still be amended. The check is differential:
+      # it compares the release share projected *with* this branch against the
+      # one projected for the base alone, so a regression already on main cannot
+      # block unrelated pull requests.
+      - name: Check the self-hosting ratchet against the merge base
+        env:
+          GITHUB_BASE_REF: ${{ github.base_ref }}
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          base="origin/${GITHUB_BASE_REF}"
+          rust-script scripts/self-hosting-metric.rs --check-ratchet "$base" --until HEAD
+
   # === DOCKER IMAGE CHECK ===
   # Issue #808: the Dockerfile was only ever built inside `auto-release` /
   # `manual-release`, i.e. after the crate had already been published to
@@ -272,7 +288,11 @@ jobs:
   lint:
     name: Lint and Format Check
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    timeout-minutes: 10
+    # Issue #812: this job used to take ~3.3 minutes; it now takes 6.8-7.8 as
+    # checks accumulated (the last three `main` runs measured 6.7, 6.8, 7.8),
+    # which is 78% of the old 10-minute cap. Raised before it produces the same
+    # "cancelled" false failure that `test` did in run 29767811026.
+    timeout-minutes: 15
     needs: [detect-changes]
     # Note: a status-check function is required because detect-changes is skipped
     # on workflow_dispatch; without one the skipped dependency would cascade and
@@ -329,14 +349,57 @@ jobs:
           restore-keys: |
             ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-
 
+      # Issue #812: nothing validated the workflow definitions themselves, so a
+      # mistyped `needs.<job>` reference, an undefined output or a malformed
+      # expression could only be discovered by pushing and watching it fail --
+      # and expression errors that evaluate to an empty string fail *open*,
+      # skipping the guarded step with a green check. actionlint type-checks
+      # `needs`/`steps`/`env` contexts and runs shellcheck over every `run:`
+      # block. Pinned by version; `.github/actionlint.yaml` declares the runner
+      # labels newer than this release knows about.
+      - name: Lint GitHub Actions workflows
+        env:
+          ACTIONLINT_VERSION: 1.7.7
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          curl -fsSL \
+            "https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint/releases/download/v${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}/actionlint_${ACTIONLINT_VERSION}_linux_amd64.tar.gz" \
+            | tar xz -C /tmp actionlint
+          /tmp/actionlint -color
+
+      # actionlint only reaches shell embedded in `run:` blocks. The standalone
+      # scripts those blocks invoke -- install.sh, check-secrets.sh,
+      # simulate-fresh-merge.sh and the rest -- had no linter at all, which is
+      # where unquoted expansions and `cd` without a guard live (issue #812).
+      # `experiments/` is deliberately excluded: those are throwaway
+      # reproduction scripts, not shipped code.
+      - name: Lint shell scripts
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          mapfile -t scripts < <(
+            git ls-files '*.sh' \
+              | grep -v -e '^experiments/' -e '^dev/log/' -e '^docs/case-studies/'
+          )
+          if [ ${#scripts[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
+            echo "::error::No shell scripts matched; the lint selector is broken" >&2
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          printf 'Linting %s shell script(s)\n' "${#scripts[@]}"
+          shellcheck --severity=warning "${scripts[@]}"
+
       - name: Check formatting
         run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
 
       - name: Verify Cargo.lock is synchronized
         run: cargo metadata --locked --format-version 1 > /dev/null
 
+      # `-D warnings` is required, not optional: every lint in `[lints.clippy]`
+      # of Cargo.toml is set to `warn`, so without it clippy printed findings
+      # and still exited 0 -- a green check over unreviewed lint debt
+      # (issue #812). The tree is clippy-clean at the time this was added, so
+      # this promotes existing behaviour rather than introducing new failures.
       - name: Run Clippy
-        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features
+        run: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
 
       # rustdoc-only lints are not exercised by rustc, clippy, or doctests.
       # Build the same public API docs deployed by Pages before release so a
@@ -472,7 +535,26 @@ jobs:
       # this dependency-free job rather than being silently skipped inside it.
       - name: Run desktop library tests
         run: |
-          node --test $(ls desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs | grep -v '/web-tools.test.mjs$')
+          set -euo pipefail
+          # Issue #812: this was `node --test $(ls ... | grep -v ...)`. If the
+          # glob ever stopped matching, the substitution collapsed to nothing and
+          # `node --test` fell back to its own directory discovery -- a green
+          # step that ran none of these tests. Build the list explicitly and
+          # refuse to pass on an empty one.
+          shopt -s nullglob
+          tests=()
+          for test_file in desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs; do
+            case "$test_file" in

+            esac
+            tests+=("$test_file")
+          done
+          if [ ${#tests[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
+            echo "::error::No desktop library tests matched desktop/scripts/*.test.mjs" >&2
+            exit 1
+          fi
+          printf 'Running %s test file(s)\n' "${#tests[@]}"
+          node --test "${tests[@]}"
 
   # === TEST ===
   # Test runs independently of the changelog check and gates on the change
@@ -487,7 +569,9 @@ jobs:
   test:
     name: Test (${{ matrix.os }})
     runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
-    timeout-minutes: 15
+    # 25 minutes, not 15: see the note on the "Run tests" step. Keep this in

+    timeout-minutes: 25
     needs: [detect-changes]
     # Note: a status-check function is required because detect-changes is skipped
     # on workflow_dispatch; without one the skipped dependency would cascade and
@@ -552,8 +636,34 @@ jobs:
       - name: Check self-AST census freshness
         run: cargo test --test unit self_ast_census -- --nocapture
 
+      # Issue #812: run 29767811026 reported this job as *failed* although every
+      # test passed -- the suite finished at 18:43:50 and `timeout-minutes: 15`
+      # killed the job 1.1 s later, during teardown. Run 29749095334 on `main`
+      # had already done the same. GitHub surfaces a timeout as `cancelled`, so
+      # it reads as an infrastructure blip rather than "the budget is exhausted".
+      #
+      # The budget is not padding: checkout + the two data checks cost ~3m15s

+      # separately -- inflating the cap silently is what this avoids.
       - name: Run tests
-        run: cargo test --all-features --verbose
+        env:
+          TEST_BUDGET_SECONDS: 1500
+          TEST_WARN_RATIO_PERCENT: 70
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          started=$SECONDS

+          elapsed=$((SECONDS - started))
+          threshold=$((TEST_BUDGET_SECONDS * TEST_WARN_RATIO_PERCENT / 100))

+            echo "::warning title=Test suite is approaching its timeout::The suite took ${elapsed}s, at or above ${TEST_WARN_RATIO_PERCENT}% of the ${TEST_BUDGET_SECONDS}s budget. Speed up the slowest tests before the job starts reporting timeouts as cancellations."
+          fi
 
       - name: Run doc tests
         run: cargo test --doc --verbose
@@ -563,7 +673,11 @@ jobs:
   coverage:
     name: Code Coverage
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
-    timeout-minutes: 15
+    # Issue #812: the same margin problem as `test`, one run away from the same
+    # false failure. Measured on the last eight `main` runs: 11.2, 11.5, 11.4,
+    # 11.9, 12.6, 12.7, 13.5, 14.1 minutes -- the worst case used 94% of the old
+    # 15-minute cap. It instruments the same suite that takes ~11m unarmed.
+    timeout-minutes: 25
     needs: [detect-changes]
     if: |
       !cancelled() && (
@@ -674,14 +788,30 @@ jobs:

   auto-release:
     name: Auto Release
-    needs: [lint, test, build]
+    # Issue #812: this gated on `[lint, test, build]` only, so `Secrets Scan`

     # Note: !cancelled() keeps this consistent with the other jobs whose skipped

+      (needs.test-agent-cli-e2e.result == 'success' || needs.test-agent-cli-e2e.result == 'skipped')
